addiere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Adds a value to a ~l:variable~ or adds two ~m:values~ and puts the result into a ~m:variable~. See elsewhere in this documentation for an explanation of what is meant by a ~m:value~. If you add to a ~m: variable~ it must already hold a numeric value, but if you assign a ~m:variable~ to hold the result of an addition it will lose whatever value it previously held.
addiere.examples: addiere 1 zu N%0aaddiere N zu Total ergibt Total

subtrahiere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Subtracts a value from a variable, or subtracts one value from another and puts the result into a variable.
subtrahiere.examples: subtrahiere 5 von Count%0asubtrahiere X von Y ergibt Z

multipliziere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Multiplies a variable by a value, or multiplies two values and puts the result into a variable.
multipliziere.examples: multipliziere X mit/durch Y%0amultipliziere X mit/durch 2 ergibt Y

dividiere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Divides a ~m:variable~ by a ~m:value~, or divides one ~m:value~ by another, putting the result into a ~m:variable~.
dividiere.examples: dividiere X mit/durch 2~lf~divide X mit/durch Y ergibt Z

leere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Assigns the boolean value ~m:false~ to a ~l:variable~, replacing whatever it previously held. See also ~l:set~ and ~l:toggle~.

beginn.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:begin~ introduces a compound statement; a block of commands that start with ~m:begin~ and finish with ~l:end~. The entire block is treated as a single statement and ~l:end~ marks the end of the compound statement block.

alias.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:alias~ lets you use one variable in place of another. Suppose you have a number of similar objects, having identical structures, where an action must be taken with just one of them, or the same action in turn on all of them. AllSpeak has arrays but in some cases these are already in use and the language does not support multidimensional arrays. In such cases you will often find that using an alias will provide you with an efficient way to perform a standard set of operations on any one of the objects.%0a%0aThe type of the ~m:alias~ variable can be anything, but for clarity it~sq~s best to use a variable of the same type as the one being aliased. To see alias in use, take a look at our [Factory Simulation](resources/ecs/sample/factory). (Copy the script into the editor window and run it.)

modul.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Defines a special kind of variable whose job is to hold information about a script module that has been run from the current script. See also ~l:run~ and ~l:send~.

callback.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] A ~m:callback~ is a special kind of variable that is used to handle events arising from other special variable types such as the ~l:showdown|k|showdown!Showdown~ plugin. See ~l:on!on {callback}~ and ~l:payload|v!payload~.
callback.examples: callback DecoratorCallback~lf~on DecoratorCallback gehe zu ProcessEvent

schließe/schliesse.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:close~ raises an ~m:on close~ event in the called ~l:module~, which is taken to be a request for it to close itself. This typically means removing all screen elements.

debug.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:debug~ is a tool for examining some aspect of your script after it has been compiled. This can be useful when trying to track down obscure spelling mistakes. Anyone wanting to build a plugin to extend the features of AllSpeak will probably need to use ~m:debug~ from time to time.%0a%0aYou can view the entire program using ~m:debug program~.%0a%0aYou can debug the single program item at a given program counter address, using ~m:debug program item {pc}~ or ~m:debug program pc {pc}~.%0a%0aYou can show the entire symbol table using ~m:debug symbols~.%0a%0aYou can look at a single symbol using ~m:debug symbol {name}~.%0a%0aYou can use ~m:debug step~ to cause the running program to output to the console the name of the script (assuming the ~l:script~ command has been given) and the program counter, for every step of the running program.
debug.examples: debug programm%0adebug programm pc 12%0adebug script%0adebug symbol Count%0adebug schritt

dekodiere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:decode~ performs a decoding operation on a text value. The default encoding translates single and double quotes into special character sequences and is mainly for use when storing HTML, JSON or other data in your own database using AllSpeak~sq~s REST server.%0a%0aSee also ~l:set!set the encoding~, which defines the type of encoding to be used by both ~m:decode~ and ~l:encode~.

kodiere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:encode~ performs an encoding operation on a text value. The default encoding translates single and double quotes into special character sequences and is mainly for use when storing HTML or other data in your own database using AllSpeak~sq~s REST server.~lf~~lf~See also ~l:set!set the encoding~, which defines the type of encoding to be used by both ~m:encode~ and ~l:decode~.

ende.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Terminates a block of commands started by ~l:begin~. These blocks behave like single instructions.
ende.examples: lege 0 in Count%0asolange Count ist kleiner als 10%0abeginn%0a  wait 1 sekunde%0a  add 1 zu Count%0aende

zweige.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:fork~ is a special kind of ~l:goto~, that causes the program to execute from the given label but also continue executing at the next command. The multitasking implied in this is handled by _**AllSpeak**_. JavaScript programmers will know that the language is single threaded, so a cooperative technique is used internally to create the illusion of true multitasking.%0a

gehe.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:go to~ and ~m:goto~ transfer control to the command at the named  ~m:label~. The command(s) following the ~m:go~/~m:goto~ are not executed at that time.

gosub.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:gosub~ is like ~l:go~ in that it transfers control to the named label. However, when the program encounters a ~l:return~ command, execution resumes at the command following the ~m:gosub~.

wenn.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:if~ tests the condition that follows. If the result is ~m:true~ then control resumes at the named label; otherwise if there~sq~s an ~m:else~ section this is executed, then the program resumes at the next instruction after the ~m:if~. (Unless a ~l:goto~ or equivalent was encountered as in the second example.)
wenn.examples: wenn Value1 ist größer/groesser als Value2 lege wahr in Result%0awenn Remaining ist 0 stoppe%0asonst gehe zu RepeatRepeatRepeatRepeatRepeatRepeat

importiere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:import~ is part of a mechanism by which programs are broken up into separate ~link:module~s that run independently. This lets you a) write smaller programs, b) separate parts of the program that don~sq~t interact much and c) include code downloaded as a plugin.%0a%0aThe ~m:import~ command sets up a list of variables that are required to be exported by the calling ~l:module~. This list must match in both number and type, though you can use different names for the variables on each side.%0a%0aBy exporting variables to another script module you are allowing it to work on them independently of your own code. References made to the variable by the called script are working on the actual variables from the parent script.%0a%0aAs a result it goes without saying that after you import a variable its value may change if the calling script goes on working concurrently with your script. This is a situation that you will usually avoid, but there are times when it is useful to be able to follow changes to a variable supplied by another script.
importiere.examples: importiere variable Count%0a  and div Items%0a  and knopf/button SortButton

index.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:index~ is a special command for array handling. In AllSpeak, all variables are arrays. Initially they only have one element but you can set an array to have as many elements as you like, using ~l:set!set the elements of {variable} to {value}~. Inside each array is the current index for the array, which you can set to any value within the number of elements in the array. The array is still used as if it were a simple variable and only the element pointed to by the index is affected by whatever you do with the array. Where multitasking is used - with ~l:fork~ or by handling an event - you must be careful to set the index before using the array if there~sq~s any chance it may have been modified by another part of the program.
index.examples: index List zu 5%0alege Result in List

negiere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:negate~ returns the value of the given variable subtracted from zero.

auf.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:on~ defines what happens when an event occurs. The core package currently handles ~m:trigger~, ~m:close~, ~m:restore~, ~m:message~ and ~m:callback~ events; other packages contain their own events and handlers.%0a%0aA ~m:close~ event is a message from a parent to its child script, telling it to finish processing. It may then remain in a pending state, waiting for a ~m:trigger~ to continue, or it may terminate itself completely. This is up to the programmer to decide.%0a%0aA ~m:restore~ event is received by a script when the browser Back button is clicked or a child script gives a ~l:history|k|browser!history back~ command. In either case, the recipient of the event is the script that is ~dq~previous~dq~ in the window state hierarchy. Scripts can do their own window state management without the need to run separate scripts.%0a%0aA ~m:message~ event is generated when a ~l:send!message~ is received by one script from another; either from the parent to the child ~l:module~ or from a ~l:module~ to its parent. See ~l:send~. Messages are a very useful way of exchanging information between modules; they often take the form of JSON structures that contain everything needed for the recipient to handle the message. The content of the message is available as ~l:message|v!the message~.%0a%0aA ~m:callback~ event is generated by certain things like the markdown processor in the ~m:showdown~ package, which uses it to notify its own extensions that they should process some part of the text. See the documentation for that package.

drucke.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:print~ evaluates what follows and writes it to the browser console. Use it for debugging.%0a%0aSee also ~l:browser:alert~%0a%0aIf the text to be printed starts with [ or {~dq~ it is assumed to be JSON-formatted and will be printed using ~m:JSON.stringify(null,2)~ so as to be more readable.
drucke.examples: drucke `The counter ist jetzt ` cat Counter

lege.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:put~ evaluates what follows and puts it into the named ~l:variable~, overwriting whatever was previously there. Note the use of ~m:cat~ in the last example, to join together several parts of a string of text.
lege.examples: lege 59 in Value%0alege Value1 in Value2%0alege `Some data` in MyData%0alege `Counting ` cat N cat ` times` in Message

ersetze.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:replace~ searches for all occurrences of a string in an item of text and replaces it with another string.
ersetze.examples: ersetze `%0a` mit zeilenumbruch in Text%0aersetze Original mit Replacement in Line

retourniere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:return~ marks the end of a subroutine - see ~l:gosub~. Program execution continues at the command following the ~m:gosub~.

laufe.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:run ~causes a second (or third, fourth...) script module to be compiled and run. The syntax is%0a%0a~m:run {variable} [with {exports}] [as {module}]~%0a%0awhere%0a~m:{variable}~ is a variable containing the full URL of the script file%0a~m:{exports}~ is an optional list of exported variables. These will need to match the list of imports in the loaded script.%0a~m:{module}~ is an optional variable holding the module reference. You~sq~ll need this if you want to send a ~l:send!message~.%0a%0aIf ~m:nowait~ is added to the end of the command, the calling script will not wait for a ~l:set!set ready~ to be given before continuing.%0a%0aIf ~m:then {block}~ is added, the block of instructions will be executed when the script is aborted or reaches an ~l:exit~ command.
laufe.examples: laufe Ticker%0alaufe Helper als HelperModule ohnewarten%0alaufe Editor mit variable MainText und variable Index und div Panel als EditModule%0alaufe MyScript dann alarm `MyScript ended`

script.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:script~ lets you name the module; useful when debugging multiple scripts.

sende.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:send~ lets you send a message to a module created using ~l:run~. The message will often be JSON-formatted and will contain complete information about what is being requested. See also ~l:on!on message~.%0a%0aThe form ~m:send {message} to parent~ sends a message to the module that launched this one.%0a%0aIf the ~m:{message}~ is omitted then a message is sent to the recipient but with no data attached.
sende.examples: sende Message zu MyModule%0asende Message zu elternteil%0asende zu MyModule%0asende zu elternteil

setze.syntax: setze {variable}%0asetze {variable} zu {value}%0asetze {variable} zu feld/objekt%0asetze {variable} zu {value} [{value}...]%0asetze der/die/das/den/dem/des elemente von {variable} zu {value}%0asetze der/die/das/den/dem/des encoding zu url/ec/bereinige%0asetze element/eigenschaft {name} von {variable} zu {value}%0asetze der/die/das/den/dem/des payload von {callback} zu Data%0asetze bereit
setze.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:set~ is probably the most heavily used command in _**AllSpeak**_. You~sq~ll find variations of it in most of the plugin modules. Here in the core package it does all the following, as listed in the **Syntax** above:%0a%0a-- assigns the boolean true value to the named variable. See also ~l:clear~ and ~l:toggle~.  %0a%0a-- assigns a value to a variable (same as ~l:put!put {value} into {variable}~).%0a%0a-- initializes a variable to be either an empty array or an empty object%0a%0a-- assigns an array of constant data to a variable. %0a %0a-- converts a variable to an array, assigning the number of elements. When used to change the size of an array the function preserves all elements that are not affected by the size change.%0a%0a-- sets the encoding/decoding method. The default is ~m:ec~, which encodes single and double quote characters and line breaks; you can also have ~m:url~, which performs standard URL-encoding and decoding, and ~m:sanitize~, which converts accented characters to their normal (unaccented) equivalents (this one has no decoding equivalent). Once set, the encoding method is kept for the session or until altered again with another ~m:set~.%0a%0a-- Sets an element or a property of a variable, converting it to a JSON string if it was previously empty. %0a %0a-- Sets the ~m:payload~ value in a ~l:callback~ variable. %0a %0a-- Set the state of a newly-~l:run~ module to ~m:ready~, which frees the parent script to continue running.
setze.examples: setze Flag%0asetze Value zu 59%0asetze Map zu objekt%0asetze Names zu feld%0asetze Squares zu 1 4 9 16 25 36 49 64 81 100%0asetze der/die/das/den/dem/des elemente von ThisVariable zu 10%0asetze der/die/das/den/dem/des encoding zu `url`%0asetze element 5 von Item zu NewValue%0asetze eigenschaft `name` von Item zu `first`%0asetze der/die/das/den/dem/des payload von Callback zu MyData%0asetze bereit

stoppe.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:stop~ causes the current program execution thread to halt. Other threads may continue and the program will still respond to events.%0a%0aBy ~dq~threads~dq~ we mean the pseudo-threads _**AllSpeak**_ uses to simulate multi-tasking. These operate on a cooperative basis, handing control from one to the next when a command such as ~l:wait~ is encountered. This enables, for example, animations to be created where several things are happening at once, such as in our [Factory Simulation](https://allspeak.software/factory/).

wechsle.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:toggle~ converts the boolean value held in a ~l:variable~ from ~m:true~ to ~m:false~ or vice versa. See also ~l:set~ and ~l:clear~.

variable.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:variable~ defines a variable, that can hold a numeric, boolean or string value. Initially it contains none of these; a value must be assigned (e.g. using ~l:put~) before the variable can be used. If a variable is defined to be an array with more than one element (see ~l:set~ ~m:the elements~), each element can hold a different type (numeric, boolean or string).

warte.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:wait~ causes program execution to stop for a given time, expressed in ~m:millis~, ~m: ticks~, ~m:seconds~ or ~m:minutes~`. A ~m:milli~ is a millisecond and a ~m:tick~ is 10 milliseconds. The default is ~m:seconds~. Only the current program thread stops; others may continue and events will still be handled.~lf~~lf~By ~dq~thread~dq~ we mean the pseudo-threads _**AllSpeak**_ uses to simulate multi-tasking. These operate on a cooperative basis, handing control from one to the next when a command such as ~l:wait~ is encountered. In practice, in a well-designed browser application threads are short enough for this task switching to be relatively rare.

solange.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:while~ tests a condition, and if the result of the test is true it executes the command (or ~m:begin...end~ block) that follows. It then repeats the test and continues to do so until the test fails.%0a%0aWhen constructing loops like this it~sq~s common for programmers to forget to bump the loop counter, resulting in a tight loop that can bring the browser - and the computer - to its knees and risk overheating of the CPU in the process. It~sq~s surprisingly easy to get this wrong during development! _**AllSpeak**_ looks out for this happening and usually stops your script before any harm can be done.
solange.examples: lege 0 in N%0asolange N ist kleiner als 10%0abeginn%0a  gosub DoSomething%0a  add 1 zu N%0aende

benötige/benoetige.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:require~ loads a CSS or JavaScript file from a remote URL into the HEAD of your document, upon which it will be immediately ready for use. A typical use is for a weather widget.

bereinige.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:sanitize~ deals with a problem when JSON data is embedded in HTML as preformatted data and gains unwanted characters such as line breaks. This command removes the unwanted characters and replaces the variable with cleaned-up data.

beende.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Causes the script to stop and release all its data.

ergänze/ergaenze.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Appends an item to a JSON array, which is is a string value held in a single variable element (not to be confused with a multi-element variable). See also ~l:sort~ and ~l:element|v~.
ergänze/ergaenze.examples: lege leer in ItemArray%0aergänze/ergaenze `First value` zu ItemArray%0aergänze/ergaenze `Second value` zu ItemArray

sortiere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This is a powerful generalized sort function that will sort any JSON array using a function supplied in the script. In the following description, refer to the example above.~lf~~lf~The sort function is called several times, each with a pair of elements to compare. These are always called ~m:a~ and ~m:b~ and are supplied as named arguments in the array variable. The function does whatever is needed in the context and places the result value (1, 0 or -1) into a ~m:v~ argument.~lf~~lf~Because the variable is a JSON string you can perform comparisons of any complexity by diving into the elements supplied. So you can do the sort on a property of the elements rather than the elements themselves.~lf~~lf~See also ~l:filter~ and ~l:append~.
sortiere.examples: variable Items~lf~  variable Arg1~lf~  variable Arg2~lf~  variable Result~lf~    ~lf~  put leer in Items~lf~  append `fish` zu Items~lf~  append `cabbage` zu Items~lf~  append `sugar` zu Items~lf~  append `beef` zu Items~lf~  append `potatoes` zu Items~lf~  sort Items mit ArraySorter~lf~  alert Items~lf~  exit~lf~~lf~ArraySorter:~lf~  put arg `a` von Items in Arg1~lf~  put arg `b` von Items in Arg2~lf~  if Arg1 ist größer/groesser als Arg2 lege 1 in Result~lf~  else wenn Arg1 ist kleiner als Arg2 lege -1 in Result~lf~  else lege 0 in Result~lf~  set arg `v` von Items zu Result~lf~  stop

filtere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This is a generalized filter function that will extract wanted values from a JSON array using a function supplied in the script. In the following description, refer to the example above.~lf~~lf~The filter function is called once for every element in the array, each with the element. This is always called ~m:a~ and is supplied as a named argument in the array variable. The function does whatever is needed in the context and places the result value (true or false) into a ~m:v~ argument.~lf~~lf~See also ~l:sort~ and ~l:append~.
filtere.examples: variable Items~lf~  variable A~lf~  variable V~lf~    ~lf~  put leer in Items~lf~  append `fish` zu Items~lf~  append `cabbage` zu Items~lf~  append `sugar` zu Items~lf~  append `beef` zu Items~lf~  append `potatoes` zu Items~lf~  filter Items mit MyFilter~lf~  alert Items~lf~  exit~lf~~lf~MyFilter:~lf~  put arg `a` von Items in A~lf~  if der/die/das/den/dem/des länge/laenge von A ist größer/groesser als 5~lf~    set V~lf~    else leere V~lf~  set arg `v` von Items zu V~lf~  stop

CORE.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The ~m:core~ package contains all the basic stuff required of any programming language: variables, control structures, values and conditionals. With these it~sq~s possible to write and run simple command-line programs.nnThe other packages all provide features related to specific areas of programming, such as for browsers, storage, comms and special things like maps and text editors.

attrappe.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This is a dummy keyword that makes a useful place to set a breakpoint when debugging at JavaScript level, for instance when writing new plugin code.

jede.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This sets up an endlessly repeating timer that ~lf~performs the same action over and over again at the specified interval.
jede.examples: jede 5 minutes~lf~begin~lf~  print `5 minutes`~lf~  add 1 zu RepeatCount~lf~end~lf~every 1 sekunde gosub zu DoSomething

trenne.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This splits a string into an array of elements in a named variable, overwriting anything previously held by that variable. By default the split is done on lines.
trenne.examples: trenne Document in Lines~lf~split Sentence auf ` ` in Words

assert.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Assert that a condition is true; raises an error if the condition fails.
assert.examples: assert X ist größer/groesser als 0 mit `X must be positive`

kopiere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Copy a ~l:variable~, ~l:dictionary~ or ~l:list~ to another of the same type.

erstelle.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Create a directory at the given path.

decrement.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Subtract 1 from an integer ~l:variable~. See also ~l:increment~, ~l:add~ and ~l:take~.

lösche/loesche.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Delete a file, or remove an entry/item/property/element from a ~l:variable~, ~l:dictionary~ or ~l:list~.

dictionary.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declare a dictionary ~l:variable~. See also ~l:entry|v~ and ~l:keys|v~.

download.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Download a file from a URL and save it to a local path.

datei/file.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declare a file ~l:variable~. Use with ~l:open~, ~l:read~, ~l:write~ and ~l:close~.

hole.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Perform an HTTP GET request and store the result in a ~l:variable~.

goto.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Jump to a labelled location in the script. See also ~l:go~ and ~l:gosub~.

increment.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Add 1 to an integer ~l:variable~. See also ~l:decrement~, ~l:add~ and ~l:take~.

input.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Read a line of input from the terminal into a ~l:variable~.

list.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declare a list ~l:variable~. See also ~l:append~, ~l:item|v~ and ~l:count|v~.

lade.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Load text content from a file into a ~l:variable~. See also ~l:save~.

lock.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Lock a ~l:variable~ to prevent modification. See also ~l:unlock~.

logge.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Print a value to the console with a timestamp and script name prefix. See also ~l:print~.

open.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Open a ~l:file~ for reading, writing or appending. See also ~l:read~, ~l:write~ and ~l:close~.

pass.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] A no-op command; does nothing. Useful as a debugger breakpoint.

entnehme.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Pop a value from a ~l:stack~ or ~l:queue~ into a ~l:variable~. See also ~l:push~.

poste.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Perform an HTTP POST request, sending content to a URL.

stapele.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Push a value onto a ~l:stack~ or ~l:queue~. See also ~l:pop~.

queue.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declare a queue (FIFO) ~l:variable~. See also ~l:push~ and ~l:pop~.

read.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Read content from an open ~l:file~ into a ~l:variable~. See also ~l:open~ and ~l:write~.

release.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Release the parent script so it can continue execution after a ~l:module~ call.

reset.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Reset a ~l:variable~ to its initial (empty) state.

save.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Save content to a local file. See also ~l:load~.

mische.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Randomly shuffle the elements of a ~l:list~ ~l:variable~.

ssh.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declare an SSH connection ~l:variable~ for remote file operations.

stack.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declare a stack (LIFO) ~l:variable~. See also ~l:push~ and ~l:pop~.

system.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Execute a system shell command. See also ~l:system|v~ for capturing output.

kürze/kuerze.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Remove leading and trailing whitespace from a ~l:variable~. See also ~l:trim|v~.

truncate.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Truncate an open ~l:file~ to zero length.

use.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Load a plugin or enable a built-in module (e.g. ~m:use server~).

write.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Write content to an open ~l:file~. See also ~l:open~ and ~l:read~.

test.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Run a test assertion. Used for internal testing.

sprache.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Declares the human language used by the script. This must appear before any other code. The ~m:{name}~ can be an ISO language code (e.g. ~m:it~, ~m:en~) or a language label (e.g. ~m:italiano~, ~m:english~). The compiler loads the matching language pack and uses it to interpret all subsequent keywords.%0a%0aIn the JS version the pack is looked up first by direct name, then by scanning loaded packs for a matching ~m:meta.label~ or ~m:meta.language~. The Python version behaves identically: it first tries a direct filename match, then scans all language pack files for a matching label.

info.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] A metadata directive that embeds descriptive text in a script. The compiler skips this command entirely; it is only used by the CLI ~m:allspeak info {script}~ command to extract and display information about a script.%0a%0aThe ~m:{text}~ argument uses the same backtick-delimited literal syntax as other string values, so it can span multiple lines. If the text is valid JSON it will be pretty-printed when displayed.%0a%0aFrom the command line:%0a~m:allspeak info~ — lists all ~m:.as~ files in the current directory.%0a~m:allspeak info {name}~ — displays the info text from the named script (the ~m:.as~ extension is optional).
info.examples: info `This script demonstrates basic Italian AllSpeak.%0aIt counts von 0 zu 4 und prints a greeting.`

wahr.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the boolean value ~m:true~. This is not often needed as it~sq~s usually implied from the context.

falsch.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the boolean value ~m:false~. This is not often needed as it's usually implied from the context.

leer.examples: wenn Text ist leer lege `default` in Text

zeilenumbruch.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] A new line character.
zeilenumbruch.examples: drucke Line1 cat zeilenumbruch cat Line2

jetzt.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The timestamp - the number of seconds since the epoch (January 1, 1970).%0a%0aSee also ~l:today~, ~l:date~ and ~l:format~.

modulo.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns one integer ~m:value~ modulo another, which is to say, the remainder after dividing the former by the latter.

eigenschaft.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:property~ is used to access an item in a JSON structure. In _**AllSpeak**_, JSON objects are ~dq~stringified~dq~ and kept in plain text variables. When you request a ~m:property~ from such an object it is first parsed then the requested property is extracted for you. See also ~l:element~ and ~l:set|k!set property~.
eigenschaft.examples: lege eigenschaft `name` von PersonalData in Name~lf~put eigenschaft ID von Record in ID

element.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:element~ is used to access an item in a JSON array. In _**AllSpeak**_, JSON objects are ~dq~stringified~dq~ and kept in plain text variables. When you request an ~m:element~ from such an object it is first parsed then the requested element is extracted for you. See also ~l:property~.
element.examples: lege element 5 von MyArray in Value~lf~put element N von Data in Item%0alege element 0 von MyList in First

zufall.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] ~m:random~ returns a random number between 0 and the ~m:value~ given.
zufall.examples: addiere zufall 10 zu Count%0asubtrahiere zufall Size von Value ergibt V

cos.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] returns the cosine of the angle given, multiplied by the specified radius. See also ~l:sin~ and ~l:tan~.
cos.examples: lege cos X radius R in Length

sin.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] returns the sine of the angle given, multiplied by the specified radius. See also ~l:cos~ and ~l:tan~.%0a
sin.examples: lege sin X radius R in Length

tan.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] returns the tangent of the angle given, multiplied by the specified radius. See also ~l:sin~ and ~l:cos~.
tan.examples: lege tan X radius R in Length

links.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] returns the leftmost ~m:{count}~ characters of ~m:{text}~.%0a
links.examples: lege links 5 von Text in Prefix

rechts.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] returns the rightmost ~m:{count}~ characters of ~m:{Text}~.
rechts.examples: lege rechts 5 von Text in Suffix

von.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Extracts part of a string, given the start and end character indices. The characters taken start at the first index and continue to one before the end index. If no end index is given, everything up to the end of the string is taken.
von.examples: lege von 8 von Sentence in Phrase~lf~put von X zu Y von `the quick brown fox` in Extract

position.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] returns the position in ~m:{haystack}~ of either the first or the last occurrence of ~m:{needle}~, where both ~m:{needle}~ and ~m:{haystack}~ are text values.%0a%0aIf ~m:{needle}~ is not contained in ~m:{haystack}~ the value ~m:-1~ is returned.%0a%0aIf the optional ~m:nocase~ is given the match is done with case ignored.%0a
position.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des position von `fox` in `The quick brown fox jumps over der/die/das/den/dem/des lazy dog` in Pos (Pos=16)%0alege der/die/das/den/dem/des position nocase von der/die/das/den/dem/des letzte `dot` in `Dot dot Dot` in P (P=8)

dekodiere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] decodes the ~m:variable~ using the current encoding (see ~l:set|k~). See also ~l:encode~.

kodiere.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] decodes the ~m:variable~ using the current encoding (see ~l:set|k~). See also ~l:decode~.%0a

kleinbuchstaben.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Converts the value to lower case; useful for string comparisons.%0a

index.syntax: der/die/das/den/dem/des index von {variable}~lf~the index von {value} in {list}
index.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The first form gets the current index of ~m:{variable}~. All variables in AllSpeak are arrays, though most only have a single element. Where they have more than one (as set by ~l:set|k!set the elements of {variable}~) the index always points to the current element and the variable acts as if only has that one element.~lf~~lf~The second form gets the index of an element in an array (list).
index.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des index von Values in M~lf~put der/die/das/den/dem/des index von `March` in Months in Index

wert.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Gets the numeric value of ~m:{value}~.
wert.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des wert von `459` in N459%0alege der/die/das/den/dem/des wert von ThisString in Code

nachricht.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This holds the value of the last message received by this module. There is no information about who sent it; if you need this you must put some appropriate content into the message itself. See ~l:send|k~ and ~l:on|k!on message~.

format.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This ~sq~stringifies~sq~ a numeric value in potentially many different ways. Only one is implemented at present, this being to format dates.%0a%0aThe ~m:{value}~ is a numeric timestamp such as that obtained using ~l:now~. The ~m:{specification}~ is a variable, formatted as in the example above, where ~m:mode~ is the formatting mode, ~m:date~ signifying that date formatting should be done, while ~m:locale~ and ~m:options~ are the parameters required for the JavaScript [Date.toLocaleDateString()](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Date/toLocaleDateString) function. The string output in this case is in this format:%0a%0a`April 21, 2020`
format.examples: setze DateFormat zu objekt%0asetze eigenschaft `mode` von DateFormat zu `date`%0asetze eigenschaft `locale` von DateFormat zu `en-EN`%0asetze Item zu objekt%0asetze eigenschaft `year` von Item zu `numeric`%0asetze eigenschaft `month` von Item zu `long`%0asetze eigenschaft `day` von Item zu `numeric`%0asetze eigenschaft `options` von DateFormat zu Item%0alege jetzt in Timestamp%0adrucke Timestamp format DateFormat

heute.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The timestamp - the number of seconds since the epoch (January 1, 1970) up to midnight (the start of) today. See ~l:format~ for an example of usage.~lf~~lf~See also ~l:now~, ~l:date~, ~l:time~ and ~l:format~.

datum.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the timestamp of a given date - the number of seconds since the epoch (January 1, 1970).~lf~~lf~The value returned will depend on the format given. The first example above returns 1561420800000, which is the UTC value for the date given, but the second and third return values for the local timezone. The last one returns GMT (which is the same as UTC).~lf~~lf~See also ~l:today~, ~l:now~ and ~l:format~.
datum.examples: lege datum `2019-06-25` in TheDate~lf~put datum `2019-06-25 00:00:00` in TheDate~lf~put datum `Tuesday June 25 2019` in TheDate~lf~put datum `Tuesday June 25 2019 GMT` in TheDate

char[acter].description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Extracts a single character from a string. Both ~m:index~ and ~m:value~ can be either symbols or values.
char[acter].examples: lege zeichen N von Text in C%0adrucke zeichen 5 von ~dq~helpful~dq~ --> ~dq~u~dq~

trenne.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Splits a value into an array of parts on the given separator. If no separator is given the separator is set to a newline.%0a%0aThe result is an array variable.
trenne.examples: trenne Text in Lines%0atrenne CSV auf `,` ergibt Items%0atrenne `1,2,3,4,5` auf Comma in Numbers

index von.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The (numeric) value of the current index of a named array variable. This is the value set by ~l:index!index {variable} to ...~.
index von.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des index von Button in Index

länge/laenge von.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Measures a text variable.
länge/laenge von.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des länge/laenge von Name in Length

uuid.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Generates a UUID - a random string with a very low probability of being repeated. This is usually enough to ensure global uniqueness for most projects.~lf~~lf~In some cases it may be easier to use a value composed of some local feature plus a timestamp, for example~lf~~lf~~m:MyEmailAddress cat now~~lf~~lf~which combines the user~sq~s email address with the current time, guaranteeing a new unique value every second (assuming only one person has the use of that email address). If the rules are kept to, these values are globally unique.

umkehre.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Reverses the given string.
umkehre.examples: umkehre `The quick brown fox jumps over der/die/das/den/dem/des lazy dog`

backtick.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the backtick (`) character.

break.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns an HTML break sequence (</br />)

hash.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Generates a hash of the supplied text.
hash.examples: lege hash `The quick brown fox` in MyHash

error.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the most recent error message from the program.

arg.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] This is a fairly obscure command that extracts the given property of a variable, returning it as numeric if it can. (See the JavaScript source of the code.js module if further clarification is needed.)
arg.examples: lege arg `count` von Value in MyArg

payload.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Retries the payload of a callback variable. See ~l:callback|k!callback~.
payload.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des payload von MyCallback in Payload

millisekunde.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the current timestamp as the number of milliseconds since January 1st, 1970.

zeit.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the number of seconds since midnight last.

jahr.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the current year, either by default from the current time or from a supplied timestamp.
jahr.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des jahr in Year~lf~put der/die/das/den/dem/des jahr von Timestamp in Year

stunde.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the current hour, either by default from the current time or from a supplied timestamp.
stunde.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des stunde in Hour~lf~put der/die/das/den/dem/des stunde von Timestamp in Hour

minute.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the current minute, either by default from the current time or from a supplied timestamp.
minute.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des minute in Minute~lf~put der/die/das/den/dem/des minute von Timestamp in Minute~lf~

sekunde.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the current second, either by default from the current time or from a supplied timestamp.
sekunde.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des sekunde in Second~lf~put der/die/das/den/dem/des sekunde von Timestamp in Second

tag zahl.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the current date (day number), either by default from the current time or from a supplied timestamp.
tag zahl.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des tag zahl in DayNumber~lf~put der/die/das/den/dem/des tag zahl von Timestamp in DayNumber

monat zahl.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the current month number, either by default from the current time or from a supplied timestamp.
monat zahl.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des monat zahl in MonthNumber~lf~put der/die/das/den/dem/des monat zahl von Timestamp in MonthNumber

args.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The command-line arguments as a JSON list. See also ~l:arg~.

bool.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Convert a value to a boolean. See also ~l:boolean|v~.

boolean.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Convert a value to a boolean (alias for ~l:bool|v~).

anzahl.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The number of elements in a ~l:list|k~, ~l:dictionary|k~ or multi-element ~l:variable|k~.
anzahl.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des anzahl von MyList in N

cwd.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The current working directory path.

datime.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Format a timestamp using a strftime format string. See also ~l:now~, ~l:today~ and ~l:timestamp~.

elemente.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The number of elements in a ~l:variable|k~. See also ~l:count|v~.
elemente.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des elemente von Record in N

entry.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Get the value of an entry in a ~l:dictionary|k~ by key.
entry.examples: lege entry `name` von MyDict in Name

files.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] A JSON list of filenames in a directory. Optionally filtered by ~m:type~.
files.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des files von `./data` in FileList

float.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Convert a value to a floating-point number. See also ~l:integer|v~.

int.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Convert a value to an integer (alias for ~l:integer|v~).

integer.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Convert a value to an integer. See also ~l:float|v~.

items.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The items (key-value pairs) of a ~l:dictionary|k~ as JSON.
items.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des items von MyDict in AllItems

json.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Parse a JSON string into a structured value. See also ~l:stringify|v~.

schlüssel/schluessel.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The keys of a ~l:dictionary|k~ as a JSON list.
schlüssel/schluessel.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des schlüssel/schluessel von MyDict in KeyList

modification.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The last modification timestamp of a file.
modification.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des modification datum von `data.txt` in ModDate

prettify.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Format a JSON value with indentation for readability. See also ~l:stringify|v~.

sender.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The name of the script that sent the most recent inter-module message. See also ~l:send|k~ and ~l:message~.

str.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Convert a value to a string.

stringify.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Convert a structured value to a compact JSON string. See also ~l:prettify|v~ and ~l:json|v~.

symbol.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Get the value of a ~l:variable|k~ by its name as a string.

system.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Execute a system command and return its output. See also ~l:system|k~.

tab.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] A tab character. See also ~l:newline~.

ticker.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] A monotonically increasing tick counter.

zeitstempel.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The current Unix timestamp. See also ~l:now~ and ~l:datime|v~.

kürze/kuerze.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] A copy of a value with leading and trailing whitespace removed. See also ~l:trim|k~.

typ.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The type name of a value (e.g. ~m:str~, ~m:int~, ~m:dict~, ~m:list~).
typ.examples: lege der/die/das/den/dem/des typ von MyVar in TypeName

uppercase.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Convert a string to uppercase. See also ~l:lowercase~.

variable.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Get the raw variable record by name.

weekday.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The current day of the week as a string (e.g. ~m:Monday~).

acos.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The arc cosine of a value. See also ~l:cos~, ~l:asin~ and ~l:atan~.
acos.examples: lege acos DY DX in Angle

asin.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The arc sine of a value. See also ~l:sin~, ~l:acos~ and ~l:atan~.
asin.examples: lege asin DY DX in Angle

atan.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The arc tangent of a value. See also ~l:tan~, ~l:acos~ and ~l:asin~.
atan.examples: lege atan DY DX in Angle

tag.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the day of the week (0-6). Optionally takes a Unix timestamp.

monat.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Returns the month (0-11). Optionally takes a Unix timestamp.

modul läuft/laeuft.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a ~l:module|k~ is (or is not) running.
modul läuft/laeuft.examples: wenn EditorModule ist nicht läuft/laeuft goto StartEditor

numerisch.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a value is numeric
numerisch.examples: wenn TheValue ist numerisch ...%0awenn zeichen N von Value ist nicht numerisch ...

gerade.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a numerical value is even

ungerade.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a numerical value is odd

größer/groesser.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if one greater is greater than another.
größer/groesser.examples: wenn Count ist größer/groesser als 5 stop~lf~If X ist größer/groesser als Y ...

kleiner.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if one value is less than another value.
kleiner.examples: wenn Count ist kleiner als 5 stoppe%0aIf X ist kleiner als Y ...

enthält/enthaelt.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a JSON list includes a specific value.

ist.syntax: ist {value}~lf~is ein/eine/einen/einem/einer feld / ist ein/eine/einen/einem/einer objekt
ist.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] The first form tests if the value given is a specific numeric or string value.~lf~~lf~The second form tests if the given variable holds a JSON array or a JSON object.
ist.examples: wenn Number ist 5 {do something}~lf~if Word ist `help` gehe zu DoHelp~lf~if Variable ist ein/eine/einen/einem/einer feld DoThis~lf~if Variable ist ein/eine/einen/einem/einer objekt DoThat

boolean.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a value is a boolean. See also ~l:numeric~ and ~l:string|c~.

debugging.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if the script is running in debug mode.

leer.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a ~l:variable|k~ is empty (has no value or is an empty string).

existiert.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a file exists at the given path.

hasEntry.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a ~l:dictionary|k~ has an entry with the given key. See also ~l:hasProperty|c~.

hasProperty.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a value has a given property. See also ~l:hasEntry|c~.

list.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a value is a list.

none.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a value is ~m:None~.

beginnt.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a string starts with a given prefix. See also ~l:ends|c~ and ~l:includes~.

endet.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a string ends with a given suffix. See also ~l:starts|c~ and ~l:includes~.

string.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a value is a string. See also ~l:numeric~ and ~l:boolean|c~.

sshError.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if the last ~l:ssh|k~ operation resulted in an error.

sshExists.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a file exists on a remote ~l:ssh|k~ host.

beginnt mit.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a string starts with a given prefix. See also ~l:ends with|c~ and ~l:includes~.

endet mit.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a string ends with a given suffix. See also ~l:starts with|c~ and ~l:includes~.

feld.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a value is an array. See also ~l:object|c~.

objekt.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if a value is an object. See also ~l:array|c~.

verfolgung.description: [Übersetzung in Arbeit] Tests if the tracer is currently active.
